codeReview.summary: The rerun resolves the prior migration/auth-family failure. `MIGRATION_3_4` no longer defaults already-orphaned hosts to password repair, the domain model can represent unresolved legacy auth family as `UNKNOWN`, and the host editor now blocks relink/save until the user explicitly chooses a credential family for those migrated orphaned hosts.

summary: Pass. I reviewed the mission guidance, prior failing review, latest worker handoff/transcript skeleton, and commit `e686cbebeb1dc246e63e3527e5f97e45e60aa9e7`. The new migration changes `hosts.authKind` to `UNKNOWN` for already-orphaned records instead of forcing `PASSWORD` (`core/data/src/main/kotlin/io/github/jtsang4/aterm/core/data/local/AtermDatabase.kt:44-64`), regression coverage now asserts that behavior (`core/data/src/androidTest/kotlin/io/github/jtsang4/aterm/core/data/FoundationRepositoriesInstrumentedTest.kt:501-519`), and repair UI leaves those hosts unresolved until the user explicitly picks an auth family (`feature/hosts/src/main/kotlin/io/github/jtsang4/aterm/feature/hosts/HostEditorModels.kt:19-59`, `feature/hosts/src/main/kotlin/io/github/jtsang4/aterm/feature/hosts/HostsEntryPoint.kt:536-652`). That directly addresses the previous failure where legacy orphaned key-auth hosts silently reopened in password repair mode.
